goal: 修复 followups 和 risks 中文截断破坏 UTF-8

summary.title: Preserve UTF-8 when truncating CLI text
summary.what: Added a shared rune-aware CLI truncation helper and routed followups, risks, status, and trace display truncation through it. Added focused tests for Chinese strings at truncation boundaries.
summary.why: The previous byte-slicing truncation could split multibyte characters and render replacement glyphs in Chinese follow-up and risk output; the same pattern existed in adjacent CLI display paths.
summary.user_goal: 修复 followups 和 risks 中文截断破坏 UTF-8

decisions.chose: Centralize CLI truncation in internal/cli/text.go instead of fixing each command inline.
decisions.rationale: The vulnerable display paths share the same behavior; one helper prevents followups, risks, status, and trace from drifting back to byte slicing.

decisions.chose: Keep followups using the existing ASCII ... suffix while applying rune-aware truncation.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the visible followups convention and fixes the UTF-8 boundary bug underneath it.

decisions.chose: Add helper-level tests with Chinese strings that exceed and do not exceed the rune limit.
decisions.rationale: The bug is caused by string boundary handling, so the helper test directly verifies valid UTF-8 and absence of replacement runes.

review_notes: Validated with `go test ./internal/cli`; local `make test` was intentionally not run per workspace guidance.

architectural_claims: CLI display truncation for user-authored text should count runes rather than bytes before appending an ellipsis suffix.
behavioral_changes: mainline followups no longer emits broken UTF-8 when truncating Chinese follow-up text.
mainline risks no longer emits broken UTF-8 when truncating Chinese risk text.
mainline status and mainline trace now use the same rune-aware truncation helper for shortened display text.
